## Purpose
This file defines a JSON schema for a manifest object, specifying the structure and validation rules for manifest data used in the application.

## User-Visible Behavior
Users or developers validating against this schema will receive feedback on whether their manifest data conforms to the defined structure, including required fields and types.

## Business Rules
The schema enforces that the manifest object must include the fields `schemaVersion`, `lastSyncedCommit`, and `files`. The `schemaVersion` must be a number with a constant value of 2. The `files` property can contain additional properties, but they must all be strings. No additional properties are allowed at the top level of the manifest object.

## Data Inputs And Outputs
Input data must be a JSON object that matches the defined schema. The output is a validation result indicating whether the input conforms to the schema, along with any validation errors if applicable.

## Side Effects
None identified.

## Error And Edge Cases
Validation errors will occur if the required fields are missing, if the `schemaVersion` is not equal to 2, or if any properties in the `files` object are not strings. Additional properties at the top level will also trigger validation errors.

## Dependencies
None identified.

## Operational Notes
This schema follows the JSON Schema Draft 7 specification. It is important to ensure that any manifest data adheres to the defined structure to avoid validation issues during processing.
